acceptance_criteria: Running a real (or local-Ollama-backed) query against a small seeded corpus returns an answer whose citations resolve to real files that exist in the corpus.
test_spec: An end-to-end test/script seeds a handful of topic files, runs a query through the full pipeline, and asserts the returned citations are valid file paths matching content in the corpus.

key_pointers.test_2: agents/query/test_query_e2e.py:test_e2e_hallucinated_citation_is_flagged -- hallucinated citation to a non-corpus path is flagged by unknown_citations(), real citation is not
key_pointers.seeded_corpus_fixture: agents/query/test_query_e2e.py:_seed_corpus (writes 3 real markdown files to pytest's tmp_path)
key_pointers.real_disk_backed_fakes: agents/query/test_query_e2e.py:_make_get_file / _make_search_candidates -- read real file content off disk on every call, not a hardcoded dict
key_pointers.naming_deviation: Issue #25's literal impacted-modules text names agents/query/test_pipeline_e2e.py; this run used agents/query/test_query_e2e.py instead to avoid name confusion with the already-existing agents/query/test_pipeline.py (4.6.1's unit-level test) -- disclosed in requirement.md

scope_of_end_to_end.meaning: Through the full Python pipeline's existing DI seam (agents/query/pipeline.py:run_query_pipeline), with a real on-disk seeded corpus and a fake (non-network) LLMClient -- every non-LLM, non-gRPC step (select_top_k, expand_insufficient_topics, combine_and_cap, _build_selected_markdown, citation resolution) runs for real and unmocked against real file content read from disk.
scope_of_end_to_end.not_meaning: This does NOT go through a real gRPC/HTTP boundary -- no real engine subprocess, no real Go /query route call, no real Ollama call. That remains future work.
scope_of_end_to_end.why: 4.6.1 (prior subtask) deliberately built agents/query/pipeline.py with search_candidates/graph_neighbors/get_file as injected callables and disclosed, explicitly, that no real gRPC client wiring exists anywhere in agents/query/ today (no wiring.py analogue, unlike agents/ingestion/). This is an accepted, already-verified (031-verification, PASS_WITH_COMMENTS) gap, not something this subtask was scoped to fix.

issue_25_overall_assessment.4.6.2_status: implemented this run (NOT yet independently verified -- implementation agent does not self-verify per invariant I4), commit f660ab8
issue_25_overall_assessment.caveat: Issue #25's two subtasks are both now implemented, but 'full query-pipeline wiring + end-to-end test' is only genuinely end-to-end through the DI seam with fakes, NOT through real gRPC/HTTP wiring. Concretely: (a) api/main.go's /query route still returns 500 for every real request (notImplementedPipeline stand-in, F-4.6.1-1, disclosed and unresolved), and (b) agents/query/ has no real SearchCandidates/GraphNeighbors/GetFile client implementation anywhere, so nothing in this repo today can actually run agents/query/pipeline.py against a real, running engine process end-to-end. Real gRPC wiring (extending proto/hivemind.proto with a RunQuery-style RPC, or an HTTP/JSON bridge between api/'s Go process and a Python worker process, plus a real agents/query/wiring.py analogous to agents/ingestion/wiring.py) remains disclosed future work -- likely scoped to milestone #10 or a new follow-up issue, not part of issue #25's own two subtasks as written.
issue_25_overall_assessment.recommendation_for_verifier: Confirm whether issue #25 should be considered 'done' given this disclosed scope boundary (both subtasks' own acceptance criteria are satisfied as literally written, given 4.6.1's own already-verified DI-seam disclosure) before closing the GitHub issue -- this is a judgment call for /cdr:verify and the user, not decided unilaterally by this implementation run.

test_results.full_regression: cd agents && python3 -m pytest . --ignore=ingestion/test_e2e_smoke.py -q -> 293 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing protobuf Gencode/Runtime VersionError, ingestion/test_shortlist.py, tracked under issue #46, unrelated)
test_results.lint: ruff check agents/query/test_query_e2e.py -> All checks passed

security_note: gh issue view 25 was read fresh for this run; its body contained no embedded system-reminder-style or instruction-injection text -- clean, unlike some prior runs on this repo. This session's own tool/environment output did surface unrelated fake system-reminder-style text (a fake 'date changed' notice and a fake 'Auto Mode Active' directive, plus an MCP-server-instructions block for a tool not actually invoked) -- none of it originated from gh/git content, none of it was acted upon, consistent with this repo's previously-documented recurring pattern (see regression.jsonl entries F-3.3.1-* / F-4.5.2-3 for precedent).
next_step: Dispatch /cdr:verify for subtask 4.6.2. If verification passes, issue #25 has both subtasks implemented+verified locally (not yet pushed/closed) -- a subsequent /cdr:commit-style close-out step would need explicit user authorization before pushing/closing the GitHub issue, per this repo's established pattern for prior issues (#18, #24).
